overview.problem: Carbide lacks structural knowledge graph operations (extract section, inline reference, rename document, delete with backlink cleanup) that iwes provides via LSP. Integrating iwes requires spawning and managing a child process, implementing an LSP client, and exposing operations through Carbide's ports+adapters architecture.
overview.approach: A generic reusable LSP client in shared/lsp_client handles stdio transport and JSON-RPC protocol. An IWE feature module in features/iwe wraps the client with Tauri commands exposing all iwes LSP capabilities. A frontend IWE feature module (port, adapter, store, service, actions) follows the standard vertical slice pattern. Two reactors manage iwes lifecycle (start/stop on vault open/close) and document synchronization (didOpen/didChange/didSave). WorkspaceEdit results from code actions are applied to disk by the backend; the frontend reconciles via the existing watcher reactor.

decisions.decision: Generic LSP client in shared/ + IWE feature in features/
decisions.reasoning: Architecture mandates shared/ for cross-feature code -> LSP client is reusable infrastructure -> iwe feature wraps it with domain-specific commands. Matches composition-over-inheritance principle.

decisions.decision: Add lsp-types crate for typed LSP messages
decisions.reasoning: iwes uses lsp-types -> typed construction prevents protocol errors -> lightweight dependency with no runtime overhead -> manual JSON is error-prone.

decisions.decision: Backend applies WorkspaceEdit to disk sequentially with per-operation error collection, watcher suppression during batch, and frontend reconciliation via watcher
decisions.reasoning: Backend is IO layer -> code action edits are file ops -> applying directly avoids round-trip. Sequential ops without rollback is simpler and matches iwes behavior (server already validated the edit). Watcher suppression during multi-file writes prevents transient inconsistent frontend state. Per-operation errors collected and returned so frontend can report partial failures.

decisions.decision: One iwes process per vault in HashMap state struct
decisions.reasoning: iwes needs base_path = vault root -> multiple vaults need independent instances -> SearchDbState uses same HashMap pattern -> lifecycle: spawn on vault open, kill on close.

decisions.decision: Stdio transport with reader/writer threads and mpsc channels
decisions.reasoning: iwes uses Connection::stdio() -> stdio simplest for child processes -> mpsc allows async Tauri commands to await responses -> matches SearchDbState worker thread pattern.

decisions.decision: Frontend IWE vertical slice, initial UI via command palette and context rail
decisions.reasoning: Architecture mandates vertical slices -> initial integration targets existing surfaces -> dedicated IWE panel deferred to avoid scope creep.

decisions.decision: Document sync via didOpen/didChange/didSave from frontend reactor
decisions.reasoning: iwes uses FULL sync -> must send entire doc on change -> frontend tracks note lifecycle -> reactor watches editor_store and triggers IWE port calls.

decisions.decision: Debounce didChange at 500ms on frontend, value not configurable initially
decisions.reasoning: iwes rebuilds index on every change -> rapid typing floods server -> 500ms balances responsiveness with performance -> similar to outline debounce at 300ms. Hardcoded for now; if iwes index rebuild proves slower/faster than expected, extract to config. No measurement baseline exists yet, so 500ms is a reasonable starting point informed by the existing 300ms outline debounce.

decisions.decision: LspClientError propagates as string through Tauri command Result<T, String>, mapped to IweStatus::Error and store.error on frontend
decisions.reasoning: Tauri commands already use Result<T, String> for error propagation in existing features -> LspClientError implements Display for human-readable messages -> frontend IweService catches errors, sets store.error and store.status=error -> typed error enum not needed since errors are displayed to user, not programmatically matched.

decisions.decision: On iwes crash: set IweStatus::Error, auto-restart once with exponential backoff, notify user on repeated failure
decisions.reasoning: Process crash detected by reader thread exit -> immediate restart covers transient failures (e.g., OOM on large vault) -> single retry avoids infinite restart loops -> user notification on repeated failure allows manual intervention -> matches resilient lifecycle SHOULD constraint.

decisions.decision: Omit didClose notification; iwes uses file watching as substitute
decisions.reasoning: iwes rebuilds its graph from filesystem state -> it watches files independently via notify crate -> sending didClose would only inform server that client stopped tracking the file, but iwes already tracks all files in vault via its watcher -> omitting reduces protocol surface without losing functionality.

decisions.decision: Queue LSP notifications during initialize handshake, flush on initialized
decisions.reasoning: User may open a note before iwes finishes LSP initialize handshake -> sending didOpen before initialized is a protocol violation -> queue holds notifications until initialize response received and initialized notification sent -> prevents race condition without blocking UI.

decisions.decision: Update specta collect_commands! in export_bindings test when adding IWE Tauri commands
decisions.reasoning: specta generates TypeScript types via a test that calls collect_commands! -> new #[specta::specta] commands must be added to this macro invocation -> forgetting this step means bindings.ts won't include IWE types -> must update src-tauri/src/tests/mod.rs.

rejected_alternatives.alternative: Pure plugin approach for iwes integration
rejected_alternatives.rejection_reason: Plugins run in sandboxed iframes, cannot execute processes or access filesystem outside vault. LSP client requires child process spawning and stdio access.

rejected_alternatives.alternative: Direct liwe Rust dependency instead of LSP protocol
rejected_alternatives.rejection_reason: LSP protocol provides loose coupling and automatic IWE updates. Direct dependency would tightly couple Carbide to liwe internals, require synchronized releases, and duplicate the graph in-process.
Deferred. Sidecar bundling adds build complexity and cross-platform binary management. Users install iwes separately for now; sidecar bundling is future work once the integration is proven.

rejected_alternatives.alternative: Replace Carbide's graph view with IWE's graph export
rejected_alternatives.rejection_reason: Carbide's Pixi.js interactive graph is strictly superior to IWE's static DOT export. IWE graph data will augment (references, backlinks) rather than replace the existing visualization.

constraints: MUST: Follow ports+adapters architecture (docs/architecture.md)
MUST: All IO through ports; adapters implement for Tauri
MUST: Stores are sync, deterministic, side-effect-free ($state classes)
MUST: Services handle async orchestration, one public method = one user intention
MUST: Reactors are only persistent observers driving side effects
MUST: Components never import services; use action registry
MUST: Cross-feature imports through index.ts entrypoints
MUST: Tests in top-level tests/ directory
MUST: snake_case file names
MUST: Use specta for TypeScript type generation on Tauri commands
MUST: LSP client must be generic/reusable, not iwes-specific
MUST: iwes uses stdio transport with TextDocumentSyncKind::FULL
MUST: Post-edit: pnpm check, pnpm lint, pnpm test, cargo check, pnpm format
SHOULD: Restart iwes on crash (resilient lifecycle)
SHOULD: Debounce document sync to avoid flooding LSP
SHOULD: Reuse existing Carbide patterns (vault_id routing, SearchDbState pattern)
SHOULD: Code actions in command palette + editor context menu
SHOULD: Integrate references/backlinks into existing bases/graph UI where natural
MUST_NOT: Break existing features (search, graph, notes, editor)
MUST_NOT: Duplicate markdown parsing (Carbide uses comrak, iwes uses pulldown-cmark - both parse independently)
MUST_NOT: Add liwe as a Rust dependency (using LSP protocol instead)
MUST_NOT: Hardcode iwes-specific logic in the generic LSP client layer

risks.risk: iwes binary not available on PATH at runtime
risks.mitigation: LspClientConfig accepts configurable binary_path; iwe_start command validates binary exists before spawning; IweStatus::Error with descriptive message on spawn failure

risks.risk: iwes process crashes mid-session (OOM, panic, signal)
risks.mitigation: Reader thread detects process exit -> sets IweStatus::Error -> auto-restart once -> notify user on repeated failure (DL-010)

risks.risk: 500ms debounce may be too slow or too fast depending on vault size and iwes index rebuild speed
risks.mitigation: Start with 500ms (informed by 300ms outline debounce), measure in practice, extract to config if needed (DL-008)

risks.risk: WorkspaceEdit translation loses operations or corrupts files (URI parsing, encoding, partial writes)
risks.mitigation: Per-operation error collection, no silent swallowing; watcher suppression prevents transient frontend state; unit tests cover all DocumentChanges operation types (DL-003)

risks.risk: Initialization race: user opens note before iwes finishes LSP initialize handshake
risks.mitigation: Queue notifications during handshake, flush on initialized (DL-012)

invisible_knowledge.system: Carbide integrates with iwes (IWE LSP server) via standard LSP over stdio. The generic LSP client is reusable infrastructure; the IWE feature module provides domain-specific wrapping. iwes maintains its own in-memory graph from markdown files and rebuilds on every document change.
invariants: TextDocumentSyncKind::FULL requires sending the entire document text on every didChange notification. Partial/incremental sync is not supported by iwes. Frontend must always pass complete editor buffer content, never deltas.

Code actions are two-phase: textDocument/codeAction returns a lightweight list (title + data), codeAction/resolve returns the actual WorkspaceEdit. Frontend must make two round-trips; never apply edits from the first response.
LSP framing uses Content-Length header followed by \r\n\r\n separator before JSON-RPC payload. Both reader and writer must implement this framing. Incorrect framing causes silent desync.
Code action data format: {key, range: {start: {line, character}, end: {line, character}}} where key is vault-relative path without .md extension. File paths must be converted to IWE keys.

iwes requires base_path (workspace root) at initialization. In Carbide this maps to the vault root directory. The root_uri in InitializeParams and workspaceFolders must both point to the vault path. Incorrect base_path causes iwes to fail silently on all file operations.

Code action resolve returns DocumentChanges::Operations which can include CreateFile, RenameFile, DeleteFile, and TextDocumentEdit operations. The backend must handle all four operation types. File URIs in the response use file:// scheme and must be translated back to filesystem paths for disk operations. URI-to-path translation must handle percent-encoding and platform path separators.
tradeoffs: Stdio transport (chosen) vs TCP socket: iwes only supports stdio via Connection::stdio(). Simpler for child process management but requires Content-Length framing and separate reader/writer threads.

500ms debounce vs shorter/longer intervals: pragmatic starting point informed by 300ms outline debounce. Shorter risks flooding iwes index rebuild; longer makes hover sluggish. Will measure and adjust.

Per-vault process model vs shared process: costs more memory but provides complete isolation. iwes initializes with single base_path so multi-vault would require upstream changes. Matches Carbide's existing per-vault state model (SearchDbState).

milestones.name: Generic LSP client infrastructure
acceptance_criteria: LspClient::new(config) spawns a child process with configurable binary_path and args
LspClient::initialize() sends initialize request and returns InitializeResult, then sends initialized notification
LspClient::request<R>() sends a typed LSP request and returns the typed response via mpsc channel
LspClient::notify<N>() sends a typed LSP notification (fire-and-forget)
LspClient::shutdown() sends shutdown request then exit notification and kills the child process
LspClient::is_alive() returns false when child process has exited
Reader thread correctly parses Content-Length framed messages from stdout
Writer thread correctly frames outgoing messages with Content-Length header
LspClientError::ProcessSpawnFailed returned when binary_path is invalid
LspClientError::ProcessExited returned when request sent to dead process
Notification queue accumulates messages sent before initialized, flushes after
cargo check passes with no errors

code_intents.behavior: Module declaration for lsp_client submodule. Re-exports LspClient struct, LspClientConfig, and error types.

LspClient struct that spawns a child process (configurable binary path + args), manages stdio transport. Reader thread reads stdout parsing Content-Length headers and JSON-RPC messages, dispatches to mpsc channel keyed by request ID. Writer thread serializes requests to stdin with Content-Length framing. Notification queue: messages sent before initialize completes are queued; flushed after initialized notification is sent (DL-012). Public API: new(config) -> Self, initialize(params) -> InitializeResult, request<R: Request>(params) -> R::Result, notify<N: Notification>(params), shutdown(), is_alive() -> bool. Uses AtomicI32 for request ID generation. Process crash detection via reader thread exit sets internal error state (DL-010).

LspClientConfig struct with fields: binary_path (String), args (Vec<String>), root_uri (String), workspace_folders (Vec<WorkspaceFolder>). LspClientError enum with variants: ProcessSpawnFailed, ProcessExited, RequestTimeout, InvalidResponse, ShutdownFailed. LspClientError implements Display for human-readable error messages (DL-009). Re-export key lsp_types for convenience.
Add pub mod lsp_client; declaration to existing shared module.
Add lsp-types dependency (same version iwes uses, currently 0.97). Add serde_json dependency if not present (for JSON-RPC message serialization).

code_changes.doc_diff:
--- a/src-tauri/Cargo.toml
+++ b/src-tauri/Cargo.toml
@@ -55,6 +55,7 @@ fuzzy-matcher = "0.3"
 specta = { version = "=2.0.0-rc.22" }
 specta-typescript = "0.0.9"
 tauri-specta = { version = "=2.0.0-rc.21", features = ["derive", "typescript"] }
+# Same version iwes uses; typed LSP message construction prevents protocol errors (DL-002)
 lsp-types = "0.97"

--- /dev/null
+++ b/src-tauri/src/shared/lsp_client/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+# shared/lsp_client
+
+Generic LSP client over stdio transport. Not tied to any specific server.
+
+## Transport invariants
+
+Every message uses `Content-Length` framing:
+
+```
+Content-Length: <byte-length>\r\n
+\r\n
+<JSON-RPC payload>
+```
+
+Incorrect framing causes silent desync. Both `write_lsp_message` and
+`read_lsp_message` implement this framing; do not bypass them.
+
+## Initialization sequence
+
+`LspClient::start` blocks until the initialize handshake completes:
+
+1. Send `initialize` request with `root_uri` and `capabilities`
+2. Receive `InitializeResult` response
+3. Send `initialized` notification
+4. Signal `ready_tx` — `start()` returns `Ok`
+
+Calls to `send_notification` or `send_request` before `start()` returns
+queue on the mpsc channel; the run loop drains them after init.
+
+## Crash detection
+
+The reader thread exits when the child process closes its stdout. The run loop
+detects this via `child.wait()` and drains pending requests with
+`LspClientError::ProcessExited`. Restart logic belongs in the feature layer.
+
+## iwes-specific usage
+
+See `features/iwe/README.md` for iwes protocol constraints (sync kind, two-phase
+code actions, workspace edit operations, root_uri mapping).

milestones.name: IWE Rust feature module with Tauri commands
acceptance_criteria: IweState manages HashMap<String, LspClient> in Mutex, keyed by vault_id
iwe_start spawns iwes with vault_path as root_uri and sends initialize/initialized
iwe_stop sends shutdown/exit and removes client from HashMap
All LSP request commands (hover, references, code_actions, etc.) return typed response structs
iwe_code_action_resolve applies WorkspaceEdit to disk: CreateFile, DeleteFile, RenameFile, TextDocumentEdit all handled
WorkspaceEdit application suppresses watcher during batch and collects per-operation errors (DL-003)
All Tauri commands use #[specta::specta] annotation
collect_commands! in src-tauri/src/tests/mod.rs updated with all IWE commands (DL-013)
didOpen/didChange/didSave notification commands work as fire-and-forget
Commands return Result<T, String> with LspClientError::Display for error messages (DL-009)
cargo check passes with no errors

code_intents.behavior: Module declarations for iwe feature: pub mod service, pub mod types. Re-exports IweState.

IweState struct wrapping HashMap<String, LspClient> in Mutex (same pattern as SearchDbState). Tauri commands: iwe_start(vault_id, app) spawns iwes for vault using vault_path resolution, sends initialize with root_uri=vault_path. iwe_stop(vault_id, app) sends shutdown+exit, kills process. iwe_hover(vault_id, file_path, line, character) sends textDocument/hover. iwe_references(vault_id, file_path, line, character) sends textDocument/references. iwe_code_actions(vault_id, file_path, start_line, start_char, end_line, end_char) sends textDocument/codeAction. iwe_code_action_resolve(vault_id, code_action_json) sends codeAction/resolve, applies returned WorkspaceEdit to disk with watcher suppression during batch and per-operation error collection (DL-003). iwe_workspace_symbols(vault_id, query) sends workspace/symbol. iwe_did_open(vault_id, file_path, content) sends textDocument/didOpen notification. iwe_did_change(vault_id, file_path, version, content) sends textDocument/didChange notification with full text. iwe_did_save(vault_id, file_path, content) sends textDocument/didSave notification. iwe_rename(vault_id, file_path, line, character, new_name) sends textDocument/rename. iwe_prepare_rename(vault_id, file_path, line, character) sends textDocument/prepareRename. iwe_definition(vault_id, file_path, line, character) sends textDocument/definition. iwe_completion(vault_id, file_path, line, character) sends textDocument/completion. iwe_formatting(vault_id, file_path) sends textDocument/formatting. iwe_inlay_hints(vault_id, file_path) sends textDocument/inlayHint. All commands use #[specta::specta] for TS type generation. All commands return Result<T, String> using LspClientError Display impl (DL-009).

Serializable response types for frontend consumption with #[derive(Serialize, Type)]: IweHoverResult { contents: Option<String> }, IweLocation { uri: String, range: IweRange }, IweRange { start_line: u32, start_character: u32, end_line: u32, end_character: u32 }, IweCodeAction { title: String, kind: Option<String>, data: Option<serde_json::Value> }, IweCompletionItem { label: String, detail: Option<String>, insert_text: Option<String> }, IweSymbol { name: String, kind: u32, location: IweLocation }, IweTextEdit { range: IweRange, new_text: String }, IweWorkspaceEditResult { files_created: Vec<String>, files_deleted: Vec<String>, files_modified: Vec<String>, errors: Vec<String> }, IwePrepareRenameResult { range: IweRange, placeholder: String }, IweInlayHint { position_line: u32, position_character: u32, label: String }.
Add pub mod iwe; to existing feature module declarations.

Add .manage(features::iwe::service::IweState::default()) to builder chain. Add all iwe Tauri commands to invoke_handler: iwe_start, iwe_stop, iwe_hover, iwe_references, iwe_code_actions, iwe_code_action_resolve, iwe_workspace_symbols, iwe_did_open, iwe_did_change, iwe_did_save, iwe_rename, iwe_prepare_rename, iwe_definition, iwe_completion, iwe_formatting, iwe_inlay_hints.
Add all IWE Tauri commands to collect_commands! macro invocation in the export_bindings test so specta generates TypeScript types in bindings.ts (DL-013).

milestones.name: Frontend IWE feature module (port, adapter, store, service)
acceptance_criteria: IwePort interface defines all LSP operation methods matching Tauri command surface
IweTauriAdapter implements IwePort by calling invoke() for each command
IweStore is a $state class with status, last_hover, references, code_actions, symbols, completions, inlay_hints, error, loading fields
IweStore.clear() resets all fields to defaults
IweService methods update store state on success and set store.error on failure
IweService.start() transitions status through starting -> running (DL-012 queue flush happens in backend)
IweService guards all operations with IweStatus check - returns early if not running
register_iwe_actions registers all actions in command palette with labels
index.ts re-exports all public types following existing feature pattern
pnpm check passes with no errors

code_intents.behavior: IwePort interface with methods: start(vault_id), stop(vault_id), hover(vault_id, file_path, line, character) -> IweHoverResult, references(vault_id, file_path, line, character) -> IweLocation[], code_actions(vault_id, file_path, start_line, start_char, end_line, end_char) -> IweCodeAction[], code_action_resolve(vault_id, code_action) -> IweWorkspaceEditResult, workspace_symbols(vault_id, query) -> IweSymbol[], did_open(vault_id, file_path, content) -> void, did_change(vault_id, file_path, version, content) -> void, did_save(vault_id, file_path, content) -> void, rename(vault_id, file_path, line, character, new_name) -> IweWorkspaceEditResult, prepare_rename(vault_id, file_path, line, character) -> IwePrepareRenameResult | null, definition(vault_id, file_path, line, character) -> IweLocation | null, completion(vault_id, file_path, line, character) -> IweCompletionItem[], formatting(vault_id, file_path) -> IweTextEdit[], inlay_hints(vault_id, file_path) -> IweInlayHint[].

TypeScript type definitions mirroring Rust response types: IweHoverResult, IweLocation, IweRange, IweCodeAction, IweCompletionItem, IweSymbol, IweTextEdit, IweWorkspaceEditResult (including errors: string[]), IwePrepareRenameResult, IweInlayHint. Additional frontend types: IweStatus = 'idle' | 'starting' | 'running' | 'error' | 'stopped'.
IweTauriAdapter class implementing IwePort. Each method calls invoke() with matching Tauri command name and camelCase params. Follows TagTauriAdapter pattern exactly.

IweStore class with $state fields: status (IweStatus, default 'idle'), last_hover (IweHoverResult | null), references (IweLocation[]), code_actions (IweCodeAction[]), symbols (IweSymbol[]), completions (IweCompletionItem[]), inlay_hints (IweInlayHint[]), error (string | null), loading (boolean). Methods: set_status, set_hover, set_references, set_code_actions, set_symbols, set_completions, set_inlay_hints, set_error, set_loading, clear (resets all to defaults). Sync, no side effects.

IweService class with constructor(port: IwePort, store: IweStore, vault_store: VaultStore, op_store: OpStore). Methods: start() sets status to 'starting', calls port.start for current vault, sets status to 'running' on success or 'error' on failure. stop() stops iwes for current vault, sets status to 'stopped'. hover(file_path, line, character) guards on status==='running', fetches hover info, updates store. get_references(file_path, line, character) guards on running, fetches references, updates store. get_code_actions(file_path, range) guards on running, fetches code actions, updates store. resolve_code_action(action) resolves action, returns workspace edit result. get_workspace_symbols(query) fetches symbols, updates store. sync_did_open(file_path, content) sends didOpen notification. sync_did_change(file_path, version, content) sends didChange notification. sync_did_save(file_path, content) sends didSave notification. rename(file_path, line, character, new_name) performs rename. prepare_rename(file_path, line, character) checks if rename is possible. goto_definition(file_path, line, character) returns definition location. get_completions(file_path, line, character) fetches completions. format_document(file_path) fetches formatting edits. get_inlay_hints(file_path) fetches inlay hints. All async methods use try/catch: catch sets store.error and store.status='error' (DL-009).

register_iwe_actions function registering actions: iwe.start (starts iwes for vault), iwe.stop (stops iwes), iwe.hover_at_cursor (gets hover for cursor position from editor_store), iwe.show_references (gets references at cursor), iwe.show_code_actions (gets code actions for selection), iwe.resolve_code_action (resolves and applies selected code action), iwe.search_symbols (opens omnibar in symbol search mode), iwe.rename_symbol (triggers prepare_rename then rename at cursor), iwe.goto_definition (navigates to definition at cursor), iwe.format_document (formats current document). Actions registered in command palette with appropriate labels.

Public entrypoint re-exporting: types, ports (IwePort), adapters (IweTauriAdapter, create_iwe_tauri_adapter factory), state (IweStore), service (IweService), actions (register_iwe_actions). Follows tags/index.ts pattern.

milestones.name: DI wiring, IWE lifecycle reactor, document sync reactor
acceptance_criteria: IwePort added to Ports type in app_ports.ts
IweStore instantiated in create_app_stores and added to AppStores type
IweService wired in create_app_context with correct dependencies
register_iwe_actions called during app context creation
Lifecycle reactor starts iwes when vault opens, stops when vault closes, handles vault switch (stop old, start new)
Document sync reactor sends didOpen on note open, debounced didChange on content change (500ms), didSave on save
Document sync reactor checks IweStatus before sending notifications - no-ops when not running (DL-012 queue is backend concern)
didClose intentionally omitted per DL-011
Both reactors registered in mount_reactors with cleanup
pnpm check passes with no errors

code_intents.behavior: Add iwe: IwePort to the Ports type, import IwePort from $lib/features/iwe.

Import IweService, register_iwe_actions, IweStore from $lib/features/iwe. Instantiate iwe_service with ports.iwe, stores.iwe, stores.vault, stores.op. Call register_iwe_actions with action_registry, iwe_service, stores. Pass iwe_store and iwe_service to mount_reactors.
Import IweStore from $lib/features/iwe. Add iwe: IweStore to AppStores type. Add iwe: new IweStore() to create_app_stores.

create_iwe_lifecycle_reactor watches vault_store.vault changes. When vault opens (vault becomes non-null), calls iwe_service.start(). When vault closes (vault becomes null) or vault switches, calls iwe_service.stop() for previous vault. Uses $effect.root pattern. Handles vault switch by stopping old instance before starting new. On crash detection (status transitions to 'error' unexpectedly): auto-restart once, set error status on second failure (DL-010).

create_iwe_document_sync_reactor watches editor_store.open_note changes and editor buffer content. Tracks last_synced_path and last_synced_version. Guards on iwe_store.status === 'running' before sending any notification. When open_note changes: sends didOpen for new note, tracks as synced. On editor content change (debounced 500ms per DL-008): sends didChange with full content and incremented version. When note is saved (is_dirty transitions false): sends didSave with content. When note closes: no didClose sent (DL-011 - iwes handles via file watching). Uses resolve_decision pattern similar to metadata_sync reactor for deterministic state transitions.

Import and wire create_iwe_lifecycle_reactor and create_iwe_document_sync_reactor. Add both to ReactorContext type (iwe_store, iwe_service). Call both in mount_reactors, push cleanup functions to unmounts array.

code_changes.doc_diff:
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src-tauri/src/features/iwe/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+# features/iwe
+
+IWE LSP integration. Wraps `shared::lsp_client` with iwes-specific commands.
+
+## Protocol constraints
+
+`TextDocumentSyncKind::FULL` — every `didChange` must include the complete
+document text. iwes does not support incremental sync.
+
+Code actions are two-phase: `textDocument/codeAction` returns titles and data
+only; `codeAction/resolve` returns the `WorkspaceEdit`. Never apply edits
+from the first response.
+
+Code action `data` format: `{key, range}` where `key` is the vault-relative
+path without `.md` extension.
+
+## WorkspaceEdit operations
+
+`codeAction/resolve` may return any combination of `CreateFile`, `RenameFile`,
+`DeleteFile`, and `TextDocumentEdit`. All four are handled in `apply_workspace_edit`.
+File URIs use `file://` scheme; they are translated back to vault-relative
+paths by `uri_to_vault_relative`. Percent-encoding and platform path separators
+are handled transparently.
+
+## root_uri mapping
+
+`root_uri` must equal the vault root directory. Incorrect `base_path` causes
+iwes to fail silently on all file operations.
+
+## Per-vault process model
+
+One `LspClient` per vault in `IweState`. Matches Carbide's existing per-vault
+state pattern (SearchDbState). Multi-vault requires independent instances because
+iwes initializes with a single `base_path` (DL-004).

milestones.name: Unit tests for LSP client, IWE store, IWE service, reactors
acceptance_criteria: IweStore tests verify all initial state defaults
IweStore tests verify each setter updates the correct field
IweStore tests verify clear() resets all fields
IweService tests verify start() transitions status through starting -> running
IweService tests verify stop() sets status to stopped
IweService tests verify operations guard on status === 'running'
IweService tests verify port errors set store.error and status='error'
Document sync tests verify didOpen sent on note open
Document sync tests verify didChange debounced at 500ms with full content
Document sync tests verify didSave on save transition
Document sync tests verify no-op when IWE status is not 'running'
Lifecycle tests verify start on vault open, stop on vault close
Lifecycle tests verify vault switch: stop old then start new
Lifecycle tests verify crash recovery: auto-restart once on unexpected error
All tests pass via pnpm test

code_intents.behavior: Tests for IweStore: initial state defaults (status idle, null hover, empty arrays, no error, not loading). set_status transitions. set_hover stores result. set_references stores locations. set_code_actions stores actions. clear resets all fields. set_error stores error message. set_loading toggles loading flag.

Tests for IweService with mock IwePort: start() calls port.start with vault_id, transitions status starting->running. stop() calls port.stop, updates store status to stopped. hover() calls port, updates store with result. hover() returns early when status is not 'running'. get_references() calls port, updates store. get_code_actions() calls port, updates store. resolve_code_action() calls port, returns result. Error handling: port rejection sets store.error and store.status='error' (DL-009). No-vault guard: methods return early when vault_store.vault is null.

Tests for document sync decision logic (extracted pure function): note open triggers didOpen. Content change triggers debounced didChange with full content. Save (dirty->clean transition) triggers didSave. Note switch triggers didOpen for new note. No-op when IWE status is not 'running'. Version counter increments on each change. No didClose sent when note closes (DL-011).

Tests for lifecycle decision logic: vault open triggers start. Vault close triggers stop. Vault switch triggers stop then start. No-op when vault unchanged. Start failure sets error status. Crash recovery: auto-restart once on unexpected error, set permanent error on second crash (DL-010).
